findings.section: What to do / Acceptance Criteria / Verification Steps
findings.issue: T13 uses `--workspace=packages/middleware` (path-based) in 4 places (lines 82, 84, 100, 129) instead of the canonical package-name form `--workspace=@universal-paywall/middleware`. T14 and T15 both use the correct form. The iteration-2 systemic-fixes canonical env table documents the correct npm invocation pattern as `npm test --workspace=@universal-paywall/middleware`. Path-based workspace resolution works today (npm resolves both forms) but the inconsistency means the Acceptance Criterion on line 100 describes a command the executor may observe succeeding locally but behaving differently in a monorepo-level CI context where workspace package names are authoritative.
findings.fix: Replace all 4 occurrences of `--workspace=packages/middleware` in T13 with `--workspace=@universal-paywall/middleware` to match the canonical form used by T14 and T15.

findings.issue: T13 Context Files lists `packages/middleware/__tests__/` as the test directory for audit purposes (`[packages/middleware/__tests__/](../../../packages/middleware/__tests__/)`). Per systemic-fix §2 (binding, iteration 2), the canonical test directory is `packages/middleware/src/__tests__/`. T14 correctly lists `packages/middleware/src/__tests__/` and `packages/middleware/src/__tests__/integration/arc-testnet-e2e.test.ts`. The stale path in T13's Context Files will cause the auditor to look for test files in a non-existent directory (`packages/middleware/__tests__/`) and potentially conclude that security-tagged tests are absent — producing false findings.
findings.fix: Update T13 Context Files line 123 to: `Code (audit — tests, to confirm security-test coverage): [packages/middleware/src/__tests__/](../../../packages/middleware/src/__tests__/), [contracts/test/](../../../contracts/test/)`

findings.issue: Line 80 states 'understand the actual implementation that landed in Waves 2, 4, 5, 6 before drawing conclusions'. Per the binding iteration-2 wave table: Wave 2 = T3 (USDC spike), Wave 4 = T5 (contract tests). The middleware implementation landed in Waves 3 (T4 — contracts), 5 (T6 — middleware pure modules), 6 (T7 — verify/settle), 7 (T8 — core+adapters), 8 (T9/T11 — unit tests/deploy). The Description header on line 20 correctly uses the new wave numbering ('Waves 5/6 — middleware pure modules + verify/settle; Wave 7 — core+adapters; Wave 8 — middleware unit tests'). Line 80 is a leftover from pre-renumbering and is internally inconsistent with line 20.
findings.fix: Replace line 80 'Waves 2, 4, 5, 6' with 'Waves 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9' to match the iteration-2 wave table and the Description header on line 20.

findings.issue: The error code `to_mismatch` (introduced by systemic-fix §4 as the canonical wire code when `authorization.to != expected vault address`, replacing the earlier `recipient_mismatch`) is not explicitly named in T13's X-PAYMENT validation audit items. T13 checks the verification logic ('to == computeVaultAddress(opts.developerEoa)') but does not enumerate `to_mismatch` in its error-code catalog check, unlike the other 13 codes which are explicitly named. An auditor following T13 literally will miss validating that this specific wire code is emitted rather than the deprecated `recipient_mismatch`.
findings.fix: Add `to_mismatch` to the X-PAYMENT validation surface bullet in T13 Description, alongside the existing checks: 'confirm `to_mismatch` (not `recipient_mismatch`) is the emitted error code when `authorization.to != computeVaultAddress`'.

findings.issue: T14 Acceptance Criteria enumerates explicit bullets for all 7 settlement failure reasons, all 4 EIP-712 tamper tests, network normalization, CREATE2 invariant, etc., but does NOT have an explicit AC bullet for verifying the `to_mismatch` wire code is tested (the `to != computedVaultAddress` path from verify.ts). T14's Description/What to do line 42 does mention `to != computedVaultAddress` as a verify.ts boundary test — so it's present in the description — but the Acceptance Criteria section doesn't call it out explicitly, meaning an executor could omit it from the report without failing any checkbox.
findings.fix: Add an explicit Acceptance Criterion in T14: '- [ ] `to_mismatch` error code verified — `verify.ts` test asserts `to != computedVaultAddress` returns 402 with `error: to_mismatch` (not `recipient_mismatch` per systemic-fix §4).' Alternatively, extend the existing '`verify.ts` boundary tests' bullet to explicitly enumerate `to_mismatch`.

findings.section: What to do / step 3 — user-spec Деплой ACs
findings.issue: User-spec line 209 (the first Деплой AC) reads `npm test --workspace=packages/middleware` — a path-based workspace identifier inconsistent with the systemic-fix §2 canonical form. T15 step 3 instructs the executor to 'walk every acceptance criterion from both specs'. When the executor walks user-spec AC #1 in the Деплой section verbatim, it will encounter and may transcribe the stale command. T15 line 46 itself correctly says `npm test --workspace=@universal-paywall/middleware`, but the user-spec it reads from is stale. This creates a discrepancy in the qa-report: the AC cited from user-spec uses the old form, while the actual command run uses the new form.
findings.fix: Update user-spec line 209 from `npm test --workspace=packages/middleware` to `npm test --workspace=@universal-paywall/middleware` to keep the source-of-truth document consistent with the canonical form. Alternatively, add an explicit normalization note in T15 step 3 directing the executor to use `@universal-paywall/middleware` regardless of the user-spec literal.

findings.section: What to do / step 3 — user-spec Деплой ACs
findings.issue: User-spec line 212 (live Arc Testnet e2e AC) states the test lives at `packages/middleware/__tests__/integration/arc-testnet-e2e.test.ts`. Per systemic-fix §2, the canonical path is `packages/middleware/src/__tests__/integration/arc-testnet-e2e.test.ts`. T15 line 46 correctly cites `packages/middleware/src/__tests__/integration/` — but because T15 walks user-spec ACs and cites them as evidence, the qa-report will contain a reference to the stale path from user-spec, which could confuse a reader or cause a CI check verifying file existence to fail.
findings.fix: Update user-spec line 212 path from `packages/middleware/__tests__/integration/arc-testnet-e2e.test.ts` to `packages/middleware/src/__tests__/integration/arc-testnet-e2e.test.ts`. This is the same root fix as the user-spec stale path — the user-spec was not updated when systemic-fix §2 canonicalized the test directory.

cross_task_notes: No duplicate heavy-resource initialization across T13/T14/T15 — all three tasks are audit/QA tasks with no shared resource ownership.
T13 and T14 correctly run in parallel (Wave 10) with no dependency on each other's outputs.
T15 correctly declares depends_on: [12, 13, 14] — all Wave 10 audit tasks must complete before T15.

The user-spec stale paths (`packages/middleware/__tests__/`) are the root cause of findings in T13 (Context Files) and T15 (AC walking). Both tasks reference the user-spec; the fix in the user-spec resolves both downstream issues.
